overview: Boros Heroic is a proactive Pioneer strategy that leverages one-drop creatures and combat tricks to trigger Heroic and Prowess abilities. The deck aims to establish early tempo and overwhelm opponents with explosive damage, primarily using Illuminator Virtuoso alongside Monstrous Rage. Consistency is maintained through card advantage engines like Dreadhorde Arcanist and efficient removal like Reckless Rage.

keep_criteria: Hands containing one-drops like Favored Hoplite or Monastery Swiftspear
Hands with early spells to trigger Heroic/Prowess abilities
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early pressure or sufficient land drops
Hands without interaction to protect threats

examples.reason: Provides immediate pressure and spells to trigger abilities, aligning with the deck's proactive game plan.

early_game: Play one-drops to force opponent answers and pave the way for larger threats
Use Defiant Strike or Homestead Courage to draw cards and trigger abilities
mid_game: Deploy Illuminator Virtuoso and pump it with Monstrous Rage or Ancestral Anger for explosive damage
Use Gods Willing to protect threats and deflect blockers
late_game: Leverage Dreadhorde Arcanist for card advantage and value
Sideboard into attrition engines like Showdown of the Skalds or Jegantha, the Wellspring for longer games

key_cards.name: Illuminator Virtuoso
key_cards.notes: Grows on its own, has Double Strike, and synergizes with Monstrous Rage for lethal damage.

key_cards.name: Monstrous Rage
key_cards.notes: Permanently guarantees Trample, increases consistency for explosive plays, and acts as a cheap Embercleave on Virtuoso.

key_cards.name: Dreadhorde Arcanist
key_cards.notes: Reuses spells from the graveyard, generates value when attacking, and acts as a removal magnet.

key_cards.name: Reckless Rage
key_cards.notes: Large scope to deal with threats, bypasses drawback with most deck creatures, and triggers abilities.

key_cards.name: Gods Willing
key_cards.notes: Protects creatures from removal and deflects potential blockers, especially in monocolored decks.

key_cards.name: Favored Hoplite
key_cards.notes: Efficient one-drop that forces quick answers and paves the way for larger threats.

key_cards.name: Monastery Swiftspear
key_cards.notes: Grows alongside other one-drops and ensures opponent spends resources early.

key_cards.name: Defiant Strike
key_cards.notes: Guarantees an extra draw while triggering Heroic/Prowess abilities.

key_cards.name: Showdown of the Skalds
key_cards.notes: Provides card advantage and grows creatures for matchups requiring a less linear approach.

key_cards.name: Rending Volley
key_cards.notes: Essential against aggressive decks and complements Reckless Rage against Greasefang, Okiba Boss.

sequencing_tips: Force opponents to spend resources on early one-drops before attacking with larger threats like Illuminator Virtuoso.
Combine Monstrous Rage with other spells to threaten lethal damage over multiple turns.
Use Defiant Strike as a cantrip to draw into answers while triggering creature abilities.
Hold up Gods Willing to protect key threats from removal or deflect blockers.
Avoid going 'all-in' against interactive decks; instead, apply steady pressure to force hasty plays.

matchups.advice: Establish tempo and force victory in as few turns as possible.
Avoid going all-in; apply steady pressure to force hasty plays and react accordingly.
Push for early tempo and close the game quickly before they stabilize.
Sideboard Rending Volley to complement Reckless Rage and deal with Greasefang cheaply.
Sideboard Loran's Escape to handle removals and sweepers, and play a more patient game.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Boros Heroic is one of the most fun and proactive strategies in Pioneer today... Starting the game with a one-drop is an excellent way to put pressure on your opponent

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Illuminator Virtuoso is our main wincondition... turns Monstrous Rage into an Embercleave for one mana.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Both ensure that the opponent needs to find a quick answer, while paving the way for larger and more efficient threats, like Illuminator Virtuoso , to attack unimpeded after we force our opponents to spend resources to deal with them.
Defiant Strike is, in essence, a cantrip that interacts with the list's proposal, and its only function is to guarantee an extra draw while triggering our creatures' abilities.

In these cases, we must look for the best route to take a few points of damage each turn, and force the opponent to react accordingly. If we can force a hasty play, we will have the perfect opportunity for a more explosive turn to secure victory.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Rending Volley is essential for dealing with the main aggressive decks... complement Reckless Rage in dealing with Greasefang, Okiba Boss
Destroy Evil is the best multipurpose card we have for dealing with Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and other bigger bombs like Cavalier of Thorns and Atraxa, Grand Unifier
prevent Mayhem Devil 's looping with Witch's Oven and Cauldron Familiar from becoming a machine gun
Spikefield Hazard... works to kill some specific threats, such as mana dorks and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben
